wddegp_utils

Functions

differences_by_dim_func(X1, X2, rays, n_order[, ...])

Compute directional differences between input arrays X1 and X2 along specified rays.

rbf_kernel(differences, length_scales, n_order, ...[, ...])

Construct the kernel matrix for the weighted directional derivative-enhanced GP.

determine_weights(diffs_by_dim, diffs_test, ...)

Solve for Kriging weights using a Weighted Coefficient Kriging model.

Module Contents

wddegp_utils.differences_by_dim_func(X1, X2, rays, n_order, index=-1, index_list=[])[source]

Compute directional differences between input arrays X1 and X2 along specified rays.

Parameters:

  • X1 (ndarray) – Array of shape (n_samples1, n_features) representing the first set of input points.

  • X2 (ndarray) – Array of shape (n_samples2, n_features) representing the second set of input points.

  • rays (ndarray) – Array of shape (n_features, n_rays) representing direction vectors.

  • n_order (int) – Maximum derivative order for hypercomplex expansion.

  • index (int, default=-1) – Index of the current submodel (used to select directional rays specific to this submodel).

  • index_list (list, default=[]) – List of indices corresponding to the training points included in the current submodel.

Returns:

differences_by_dim – List containing the directional differences for each feature dimension.

Return type:

list of ndarray

wddegp_utils.rbf_kernel(differences, length_scales, n_order, n_bases, kernel_func, der_indices, powers, index=-1, index_list=[])[source]

Construct the kernel matrix for the weighted directional derivative-enhanced GP.

Parameters:

  • differences (list of ndarray) – Precomputed pairwise differences by dimension.

  • length_scales (ndarray) – Array of length scales (hyperparameters).

  • n_order (int) – Maximum derivative order.

  • n_bases (int) – Number of OTI bases.

  • kernel_func (callable) – Kernel function evaluated over differences.

  • der_indices (list) – Indices indicating derivative orders.

  • powers (list) – Powers of each term based on OTI basis structure.

  • index (int, default=-1) – Dummy argument for compatibility.

  • index_list (list, default=[]) – List identifying index subsets for directional derivatives.

Returns:

K – Fully assembled kernel matrix including derivatives.

Return type:

ndarray

wddegp_utils.determine_weights(diffs_by_dim, diffs_test, length_scales, kernel_func)[source]

Solve for Kriging weights using a Weighted Coefficient Kriging model.

This method solves an augmented linear system that enforces unbiasedness and ensures the interpolation is consistent with the weighted directional derivative-enhanced GP structure.

Parameters:

  • diffs_by_dim (list of ndarray) – Differences between training points.

  • diffs_test (list of ndarray) – Differences between training points and test point.

  • length_scales (ndarray) – Kernel length scales.

  • kernel_func (callable) – Kernel function evaluated over pairwise differences.

Returns:

w – Optimal weights vector for Weighted Coefficient Kriging interpolation.

Return type:

ndarray
